title: caryophyllata montana
intro: Caryophyllata montana, also known as sweet woodruff, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of coumarins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Caryophyllata montana are believed to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, while also exhibiting antispasmodic and antiseptic effects. Sweet woodruff can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used to soothe digestive issues and calm the nervous system.
